title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_61 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 61 the old low barrier B = {1,19,23,29} is no longer viable because its forced spill count is 39 > 20, while a zero-spill high barrier B = {1,163,167,173} works cleanly. The pure complementary prime pool is exactly one label short for C, but a single harmless supplement 121 closes the gap. This points toward a complementary-support slice rather than a pure-prime C-block slice.
candidate_theorem_slice: A four-class sufficient-condition slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): choose B = {1,b1,b2,b3} with each nontrivial bi > (5p + 19)/2, choose A from a fixed small smooth palette, and choose C from p - 1 labels whose prime factors avoid both the A-support and the barrier primes, allowing a tiny composite supplement when the pure prime pool is short.
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_61 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification on the full 324-vertex ring graph. This is still only an instance result, but it materially sharpens the active F25 campaign because it isolates the first clean zero-spill high-barrier case where the pure complementary prime pool is one label short and a single composite supplement 121 closes the gap.
next_action: Promote the verified p = 61 zero-spill / one-supplement witness into the family dossier, then run generalize before testing z59-z25 as the nearest backward comparison or z67-z25 as the next forward continuation; do not escalate this instance alone to Lean yet.
